Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights And For Order Directing Transfer Of Estate Property
Off Calendar May-16-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker and Justin Goodwin for Amy Harrington. Rosemary Gervais appeared by telephone in Propria Persona. James Collins appeared by telephone in Propria Persona. Patrick Collins in Propria Persona.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to validity of the birth certificates presented by Mr. Boessenecker, whether Patrick and Earl are the Sons of Earl Patrick Collins, spoke of the Family relationships and the Father's will. Mr. Boessenecker is to file the verification to Ms. Harrington's supplement. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Jun-21-2010 Continued To Jul-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Justin Goodwin for Amy Harrington.

The Court stated that there was a procedural issue with notice to Clara Sperling. Mr. Goodwin asked the Court to waive notice to Ms. Sperling stating that she was represented by Counsel, that he had been communicating with opposing Counsel and that waiver of notice was in pleading. The Court denied Mr. Goodwin's request stating that this was the first petition and that probate code states that Ms. Sperling is entitled to direct notice. The matter was continued to July 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-08-2010 Continued To Mar-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Continued To Mar-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator. Justin Goodwin and John Boessenecker specially appearing for Edward Collins. James Collins also present.

Mr. Boessenecker stated that they would try to work out the genealogy issues without bothering the Court, that the Father of the Petitioner is deceased and that Edward Collins qualifies for a $20,000.00 bond. In addition, he stated that Mr. Goodwin would maintain the check book for the $20,000.00, that the rest of the assets would be placed into a blocked account, and that he did not know if Mr. Collins was bondable for the full amount. The Court stated that it preferred a bond for the full amount of $580,000.00, that once the letters and the bond were issued, that then the bond could be reduced and that the rest of the proceeds could be placed into a blocked account. The matter was continued to March 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if Edward Collins is bondable for the full amount. Mr. Ferdon is not required to appear on that date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Sum.
